subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly identifies and repeatedly applies the concept of change across multiple analyses
Weaves detailed company-specific data (fleet, revenues, staffing) into discussion of change
Demonstrates clear analytical thinking linking change to strategic decision points
weaknesses: Lacks explicit definition of change dimensions (pace, magnitude, management)
Concept not used as a driving analytical lens in every section
No reference to established change-management models to deepen integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student achieves a solid, company-specific use of the key concept (band 3), the depth and consistency of integration merit a higher mark. Company data is well used to frame âchangeâ in each tool, but the analysis would be strengthened by defining change dimensions and referencing theory. Overall, the work sits between bands 3 and 4, and is awarded a 4 to reflect its mostly clear analytical integration.

strengths: Includes the optimal number of supporting documents (five)
References a mix of primary (annual report, CEO interview) and secondary sources
Documents support core calculations and context setting
weaknesses: Reliance on Wikipedia-style citations weakens academic rigor
Citations lack full publication details and consistent formatting
Depth of some sources (press articles) is uneven
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the quantitative requirement for documents and draws relevant evidence to support analysis (band 4 for number, band 3 for depth). However, uneven authority and incomplete citation details limit the depth, placing this squarely at band 3.

strengths: Employs three standard but appropriate tools (SWOT, break-even, force-field)
Each tool is defined and applied directly to Ryanairâs context
Quantitative and qualitative tools cover different dimensions of the question
weaknesses: Selection of tools is adequate rather than innovative
Assumptions underpinning tools are sometimes weak or unexplained
Narrative occasionally fails to link tool outputs back to strategic recommendations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The choice and application of tools are adequate and relevant (band 3). The student shows clear understanding but does not demonstrate the breadth or depth of application required for a top-band mark.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Broad selection of quantitative inputs (fuel, lease rates, staffing) integrated into break-even analysis
Qualitative insights from SWOT and force-field are balanced against numerical findings
Recommendation is grounded in a synthesis of different data streams
weaknesses: Calculation errors and inconsistent currency notation undermine precision
Key assumptions are not presented in a summary table
Variable definitions and formula steps are sometimes unclear
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis moves beyond description to a mostly effective, integrated evaluation of Ryanairâs long-haul decision. Errors and presentation issues prevent a fully thorough band 5, but the sustained integration and clear use of data justify a band 4.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question
Aligns with qualitative findings from SWOT and force-field analysis
weaknesses: Fails to incorporate key quantitative break-even result to justify the recommendation
Overemphasizes certain factors (staff costs, complexity) at the expense of balanced evidence
Lacks reflection on sensitivity or alternative scenarios
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion clearly addresses the research question (band 3) but only partially aligns with all evidence streams, particularly the quantitative break-even analysis. This partial consistency places it at band 2.

strengths: Logical organization: clear introduction, body, conclusion
Well-structured table of contents with coherent headings
Pagination and sectioning guide the reader effectively
weaknesses: LaTeX or special-character formatting in the TOC is distracting
Minor mismatches between headings and page numbers
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates appropriate and consistent structure throughout (band 2), with only minor formatting inconsistencies preventing a perfect mark.

strengths: All required elements present: title page, word count, in-text citations, bibliography
Consistent use of in-text citations and a comprehensive reference list
weaknesses: Inline URLs and inconsistent bibliography formatting reduce professionalism
Citation style varies between entries
Complex inline equations hinder readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met to a good standard (band 2). Formatting inconsistencies in references and equations are minor and do not detract from overall clarity.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure (introduction, analyses, conclusion) and includes a table of contents. Headings and pagination are coherent, so âappropriate structure throughoutâ (Band 2) is satisfied.

The documents (survey results, Ryanair annual report extracts, Eurocontrol statistics, CEO interview, cost tables) are relevant to the research question and support core calculations. Depth, however, is uneven: Wikipedia pages and press articles are used in place of more authoritative industry sources, and several citations are incomplete. Overall they provide ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ evidence, matching Band 3.

Required elementsâtitle page details, word count, contents list, in-text citations and full bibliographyâare all present. Minor citation formatting issues do not amount to missing elements, so the highest band (Band 2) is appropriate.

Five supporting documents (labelled #1â#5) are referenced, fitting the required range of 3â5. This number sits at the upper end of the acceptable bracket, so the highest band for ânumber of documentsâ is appropriate.

Each tool is applied directly to Ryanair and the research question: the break-even calculates a threshold ticket price, the SWOT lists firm-specific factors, and the force-field assigns weights. Nevertheless, heavy reliance on assumptions (e.g., 5 % market share, 135 aircraft) weakens relevance, and the analytical narrative does not always link results back to strategic recommendations. This fits ârelevance not always clear but adequately appliedâ (Band 3).

The student sources a variety of quantitative inputs (fuel burn, lease rates, salary data, fleet numbers) and uses them to build a break-even model. Selection is broad enough to move beyond superficial treatment, yet several figures are inconsistent, and conversions or currency symbols are sometimes muddled. This places the work at âadequate selection and use of dataâ (Band 3).

Analysis goes beyond description: cost items are projected, forces are weighted, and strategic pros and cons are balanced before a recommendation is made. However, calculations contain errors (e.g., mis-labelled currencies, unclear EBIT step) and integration of qualitative and quantitative insights is uneven. Thus the analysis is âsomeâ/âmostly effectiveâ rather than âthoroughâ, meriting Band 3.
The final section explicitly answers the research question (âRyanairâs management shouldnât operate long-haul flightsâ), fulfilling the top-band descriptor for this strand.

The IA employs three standard tools (SWOT, break-even, force-field). This constitutes an adequate, but not especially varied, selection that covers both qualitative and quantitative dimensions. Hence Band 3 (âadequate selectionâ) is appropriate.

The conclusion aligns with many, but not all, findings; for instance, it cites operational complexity and staffing cost as decisive, consistent with the SWOT and force-field, yet places little weight on the break-even result that suggested potential profitability. This partial alignment fits Band 2.

Detailed company-specific information (fleet composition, ancillary-revenue proportion, staff numbers, marketing spend, etc.) is woven into each tool, demonstrating a strong link to Ryanair. The student adjusts cost figures using Ryanairâs own FY24 data and refers to its ordering of 300 B737-MAX aircraft. While some figures are speculative, the analysis repeatedly anchors arguments in Ryanairâs realities, satisfying âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ. Full, consistent integration across every section is not yet evident, so Band 5 is not justified.

The student clearly identifies the key concept of change and links it to Ryanairâs potential strategic shift into long-haul operations. Throughout the IA the word âchangeâ re-appears in the SWOT, break-even and force-field analyses, showing that the student is aware of the conceptual focus and uses it to frame arguments. Nevertheless, the concept is treated more as background context than as an analytical lens; there is little discussion of how the magnitude, pace or management of change might influence Ryanairâs decision. Because the concept is applied in several sections but not deeply integrated into every stage of the analysis, the work aligns best with the descriptor of âgood understanding with clear analytical thinkingâ (Band 3).

annotations.comment: The research question is articulated clearly and aligns with the scope of a Business Management IA, offering a focused basis for analysis.
The concept âChangeâ is stated but remains superficial. The student should define dimensions (pace, magnitude, management) and frame analysis explicitly around these aspects.
Consider embedding the concept of change in the introduction by discussing how organizational change theories (e.g., Lewinâs model) inform Ryanairâs strategic shift.
The table of contents is well organized, guiding the reader logically through the reportâs sections and page references.
The LaTeX formatting of page numbers (e.g., \overline{\mathbf{3}}) in the TOC may confuse readers. Use plain numerals to ensure consistency.
Ensure the headings and page numbers in the TOC match the final layout; avoid OCR artifacts or special symbols that may lead to misinterpretation.
The introduction draws on generic market data but relies heavily on Wikipedia-style citations, which can weaken academic rigor. Replace with authoritative industry sources.
Deepen the integration of the concept of change by discussing how shifts in consumer behavior or regulatory environments drive Ryanairâs strategic options.
An inline URL is printed in the body rather than formatted as a footnote or reference. Reformat as an in-text citation with a corresponding bibliography entry.
The SWOT tool is introduced clearly, but its relevance to the research question could be strengthened by explicitly linking each quadrant back to the long-haul decision.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The SWOT factors listed in the matrix lack quantifiable backing (e.g., exact figures on operational complexity). Augment with data to elevate analysis.
Integrate the concept of change into the SWOT by assessing the strength of driving vs. restraining forces related to organizational change.
The opportunity âpeopleâs desires to travelâ is asserted but unsupported by data. Cite specific survey or Eurocontrol figures to substantiate this claim.
References to supporting documents (#1, #3, #4) are helpful for sourcing external data. Ensure all footnotes include full publication details.
Before the break-even section, cross-reference SWOT opportunities and threats to create a coherent flow between qualitative and quantitative analyses.
The break-even analysis lists assumptions narratively but lacks a summarizing table. Present key assumptions (market share, flights per day) in a table for clarity.
The student comprehensively lists fixed cost categories, demonstrating an understanding of cost structure relevant to the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Include a concise table of each cost assumption with its source and rationale to improve transparency and ease of verification.
The notation Atpapd is introduced without definition. Define each variable in the break-even formula to prevent ambiguity.
Currency symbols interchange between â¬ and sterling sign; standardize to a single currency or clearly state exchange rates.
Perform a sensitivity analysis around the 5% market share assumption to demonstrate how different scenarios affect break-even thresholds.
The fuel cost calculation multiplies by â2â without explanation. Document every multiplier (e.g., round trips) to ensure reproducibility.
The EBIT calculation applies tax by division rather than subtracting tax expense. Revise to calculate post-tax EBIT correctly.
Break the complex inline math into sequential, numbered display equations to improve clarity and guide the reader through each computation.
The force-field analysis tool is defined clearly, outlining how weights reflect impact on the decision, which supports structured evaluation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The force-field table omits rationale for individual force scores. Include brief justifications for each score to enhance analytical depth.
Link the force-field weights back to Ryanairâs capacity for change by discussing how organizational readiness influences driving vs. restraining forces.
The conclusion restates key findings but omits discussion of break-even results. Explicitly reference the calculated threshold (â¬354.51) to justify recommendations.
The final recommendation aligns with survey and SWOT findings but downplays quantitative profitability indicators. Balance conclusion with all evidence streams.
Bibliography entries are comprehensive but inconsistent in style and indentation. Adopt a single referencing style (APA, Harvard) and apply uniformly.
